evals.prompt: Review the skill at skills/confluent-skill-reviewer/evals/mock-skills/good-skill/ ‚Äî I'm about to ship it.
evals.expected_output: A single Markdown report with zero Blocking findings and the recommendation 'approve'. No false positives flagged.
assertions: Report's `Blocking` section shows `(0)` ‚Äî zero blocking findings
Final recommendation is `approve` (NOT `request changes`)
Does NOT flag the SKILL.md as inlining references (the lazy-load preamble is present)

Does NOT flag the `description:` as missing an anti-trigger clause (`Do NOT trigger for self-managed Apache Kafka, schema registration, Terraform generation, or Kafka Streams topology authoring.` is present)
Confirms which mode it entered (Single-skill) in the report header
Notes `skill-validator` availability status (installed or not installed) in the report header

evals.expected_output: A Blocking finding citing CLAUDE.md's lazy-load rule, identifying inlined content from references/exporters.md.
assertions: Emits at least one `Blocking` finding under Phase B (Confluent conventions / lazy-load)
Cites `CLAUDE.md` ¬ß Skill anatomy (lazy-load references bullet)
Identifies the duplicated content between `SKILL.md` and `references/exporters.md` (the kafka-exporter / Confluent Control Center comparison)
Suggests moving the content out of SKILL.md and routing to `references/exporters.md` from a decision point
Final recommendation is `request changes`

evals.prompt: Audit skills/confluent-skill-reviewer/evals/mock-skills/trigger-overlap/ ‚Äî its description feels too broad.
evals.expected_output: Flags missing 'Do NOT trigger for...' clause; cross-references the keyword overlap with good-skill (both about topic provisioning); suggests an anti-trigger that names good-skill's domain.
assertions: Emits a `Blocking` or `Warning` finding from Phase C (trigger overlap) ‚Äî NOT silent
Names `good-skill` as the colliding peer
Identifies that `trigger-overlap`'s `description:` has no `Do NOT trigger for` clause
Proposes wording for an anti-trigger clause that explicitly hands off to the other skill (`Do NOT trigger for ...`)
Cites the overlapping keywords (e.g. provision, retention, compaction, idempotent) ‚Äî NOT just domain words like `Confluent` or `Kafka`

evals.prompt: Validate skills/confluent-skill-reviewer/evals/mock-skills/bad-frontmatter/ ‚Äî something is off about it.
evals.expected_output: Blocking findings for uppercase/underscore name, name/directory mismatch, and a too-vague description; cites agentskills.io spec.
assertions: Flags `name: Bad_Frontmatter` as a Blocking spec violation (uppercase and underscores not allowed)
Flags the name/directory mismatch (`name` is `Bad_Frontmatter`, directory is `bad-frontmatter`)
Flags `description: Helps with stuff.` as too vague for triggering
Cites the Agent Skills spec (`agentskills.io/specification`) or `references/spec-conformance.md` for the name rules
Final recommendation is `request changes`

evals.prompt: Look at skills/confluent-skill-reviewer/evals/mock-skills/stale-expectations/ ‚Äî I'm worried the evals aren't pulling their weight.
evals.expected_output: Phase D flags the non-standard `expectations` key as Blocking, plus aspirational expectations, an abstract prompt, and a missing fixture path.
assertions: Flags use of the `expectations` key as Blocking ‚Äî the repo standardized on the `assertions` key, so it must be renamed
Flags the object-form assertion in eval id 2 as Blocking ‚Äî assertions must be plain strings, not `{id, type, description, ...}` objects
Reports a Blocking finding for the missing fixture path `evals/fixtures/missing-fixture`
Flags `Build me a thing.` as a Warning (abstract prompt, no domain detail)
Flags expectations like `Generates a working app`, `Includes error handling`, `Asks the right questions`, `Is well written` as Warnings (aspirational, not verifiable)
Cites CLAUDE.md ¬ß Evals are the contract (`expectations encode hard-won correctness ‚Äî treat them as regression tests, not aspirations`)
Suggests concrete replacements that name files, classes, config keys, or NOT clauses

evals.prompt: Review my current branch ‚Äî I've added a new skill and want a pre-merge check before requesting reviewers.
evals.expected_output: Detects PR-diff mode, runs phases A‚ÄìE, includes the PR-template checklist (docs, 90% evals, SME, DTX/DevRel) in the report.
assertions: Reports `Mode: PR-diff` in the report header
Runs `git diff main...HEAD -- skills/` or `gh pr diff` to determine scope
Emits a `## PR-template checklist` section at the bottom of the report
Checklist enumerates: docs updated, evals at 90%+, SME reviewer, DTX/DevRel reviewer
For a newly added skill, flags missing README.md skill-table row as a Blocking finding (when `git diff main...HEAD -- README.md` shows no change)
Does NOT review files outside `skills/` (the `Makefile`, `service.yml`, `.claude-plugin/` are explicitly out of scope)

evals.prompt: Run a full audit on skills/confluent-skill-reviewer/evals/mock-skills/good-skill ‚Äî assume skill-validator is installed.
evals.expected_output: Invokes scripts/run_skill_validator.sh, parses JSON results, maps level=error to Blocking and level=warning to Warning.
assertions: Invokes `scripts/run_skill_validator.sh` (NOT raw `skill-validator` ‚Äî uses the wrapper so `--allow-dirs=evals` is applied)
Parses the JSON output's `results[]` array
Maps `level=error` entries to Blocking and `level=warning` to Warning
Includes `validator: installed (v...)` in the report header
Does NOT propagate the validator's exit code as a blocking signal on its own (severity comes from `level`, not exit code)

evals.prompt: Audit skills/confluent-skill-reviewer/evals/mock-skills/good-skill when skill-validator is NOT installed on this machine.
evals.expected_output: Wrapper script exits 0 with install hint on stderr; the review continues with native spec checks per references/spec-conformance.md.
assertions: Does NOT abort the review when `scripts/run_skill_validator.sh --probe` returns non-zero
Notes `validator: not installed` in the report header as a Warning, NOT Blocking
Falls back to native spec checks listed in `references/spec-conformance.md` (frontmatter parse, name regex, directory match, body line count, link resolution, code-fence balance)
Includes the install hint (`brew tap agent-ecosystem/tap && brew install skill-validator` or `go install ...`) in the report
Still emits a final recommendation (does NOT refuse to review)
